risk_if_not_implemented: Without Third-Party Deficiency Remediation, unpatched vulnerabilities accumulate, providing attackers with known and exploitable entry points.

compensating_control_1.name: Risk Remediation
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to remediate risks to an acceptable level.
compensating_control_1.justification: Risk Remediation (RSK-06) provides vulnerability management that compensates for the absence of Third-Party Deficiency Remediation (TPM-09) by reducing the exploitable attack surface by addressing known weaknesses before they are leveraged. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Third-Party Contract Requirements
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to require contractual requirements for applicable security, compliance and resilience requirements with third-parties, reflecting the organization's needs to protect its Technology Assets, Applications, Services and/or Data (TAASD).
compensating_control_2.justification: Third-Party Contract Requirements (TPM-05) provides third-party oversight that compensates for the absence of Third-Party Deficiency Remediation (TPM-09) by extending security obligations contractually and monitoring third-party risk in lieu of direct control. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
